strategy: Moon boss (15,350). She resists almost everything - Storm/Myth/Balance 60%, Ice/Death 73% - and boosts only to Fire and Life (+5%). The guide's recommended plan for most teams is hit-spamming, not a big alpha strike.
Pack ONLY single-hit spells and target the boss. AoE hits, Feints (double Cleanse Ward), DoTs (Cooled down), and any non-hit action all make her Tower Shield herself.
Rounds 1-5 the Awful Snowman Taunts, forcing your hits onto it. There is no penalty for passing, so simply pass and wait the Taunt out rather than triggering shields.

Each round she marks one random wizard as the Chosen One with a 1-round Icewind aura (+15% universal damage and pierce). Only that wizard should act, and they must ATTACK - a non-Chosen wizard hitting her triggers Celestial Intervention (she heals and strips shields).

From round 6 on, single-target the Snow Queen. When the Chosen wizard hits her she hands back a +100% Aegis-protected Balanceblade; bank those free blades. The guide killed her in about 5 hits letting the blade cheat stack the damage.

Keep a key charm under Aegis so her Blizzard cannot strip it. After she dies, AoE the remaining Snowmen. Alternative for a loaded team: an alpha strike with pierce blades, auras, bubbles, Shrike and ~150% pierce - but it is riskier since most non-single-hit setup draws the triple -100% Tower Shields.
cheatNotes: A late joiner makes her summon another Awful Snowman. Be in on round 1.
Each round she marks a random wizard as Chosen with a 1-round Icewind aura. That wizard is the only one who should act this round.
Feint her and she Cleanse-Wards twice, beating Indemnity. Do not Feint; use blades instead.
Her Snowmen can Mega Taunt for 5 rounds, forcing your hits onto them. Clear taunts or wait them out.
If a wizard who is not Chosen attacks her, she Celestial Interventions, healing and stripping shields. Only the Chosen wizard should hit.
When the Chosen wizard attacks her, she gives that wizard a +100% Aegis-protected Balanceblade. Bank these free blades toward your kill.
An all-enemies attack makes her Tower Shield herself. Use single-target hits.
Single-target on a Snowman also gives her a Tower Shield. Focus her, not the snowmen, unless taunted.
Dark Pact also triggers the Tower Shield. Avoid it here.
DoTs on her get Cooled down. Skip damage-over-time.
If the Chosen wizard does anything but hit, or any non-Chosen wizard casts, she Tower Shields. Off-turn wizards should pass.
Death drain spells do not count as a hit for the Chosen rule, so they still trigger a Tower Shield. Use direct attacks.
There is no penalty for passing, so non-Chosen wizards should simply pass rather than risk a shield.
Pet and wand May Cast procs also count as a non-Chosen action and shield her. Be mindful of proc pets.
Her Blizzard strips all positive charms from your team (or wards from hers). Aegis protects your key charms from it.
Aegis stops the Blizzard from taking a protected charm. Shield your main blade before she casts it.
